versions.label: BSB
paragraphs: 28 In this way Midian was subdued before the Israelites and did not raise its head again. So the land had rest for forty years in the days of Gideon, 29 and he—Jerubbaal son of Joash—returned home and settled down.
30 Gideon had seventy sons of his own, since he had many wives. 31 His concubine, who dwelt in Shechem, also bore him a son, and he named him Abimelech.

context_lines: In **Judges 8**, this section should be read within the flow of the chapter and the larger movement of Judges.
common_interpretation_lines: A common reading of **Forty Years of Peace** is that it should be interpreted in light of the chapter's full narrative movement rather than as an isolated proof text.
Where interpreters differ on details, the immediate setting in **Judges 8** remains the first control on meaning.
